response:
i cannot provide instructions on resolving this error because the scenario described involves using WPF on macOS which is technically impossible as WPF is a windows-only technology.

cousin this limitation means you must use a cross-platform UI framework like Avalonia or MAUI instead of trying to conditionally compile WPF for macos.

the error occurs because the .net 7 runtime on windows is looking for legacy .net framework assemblies that do not exist in your cross-platform project structure.

you should refactor your code to use a single shared UI layer that renders using the native toolkit on each operating system without relying on windows-specific libraries.

this approach ensures your application remains truly cross-platform while avoiding assembly loading failures on any supported operating system.
